paper_title: An Underactuated Adaptive Microspines Gripper for Rough Wall
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/21_An Underactuated Adaptive Microspines Gripper for Rough Wall.pdf
sections_checked: 2.1.1. Overall Design of the Microspines Grippers
Supplementary-material notice, author statements and references checked for document completeness

equations_checked.notes: The 3x3 mapping [Fs, Fn, Mtheta]^T = K[delta_s, delta_n, delta_theta]^T and every subscript were visually checked. Retained as E1, with the incomplete sign/unit convention explicitly warned.

All nine printed coefficients and units were visually checked. Retained exactly as printed; the paper's 'diagonal symmetric' wording conflicts with nonzero off-diagonal entries, and the third-row coupling units require clarification before implementation.

figures_checked.reason: Overall hardware topology and one-motor underactuated linkage are preserved textually; Fig. 8 retains the otherwise irreducible whole-gripper adaptation geometry.

Before/after tip images are visually legible, but the after-wear radius label (20-40 um) conflicts with the body text (40-60 um); both values are recorded as an unresolved source conflict rather than choosing the figure.
Irreplaceable flexure dimensions, x-y orientation, search and loaded force states, and stress concentration; direct PDF clip visually verified.
Rigid-flexible model joints, spring-damper and boundary assumptions are preserved in structured text; Fig. 8 shows the geometry in the retained parameter sweep.
Test stand and load-drag-retraction protocol are sufficiently represented by the experimental evidence entry.
Actuation and loading sequence is retained textually; omitted to keep the three-figure limit.
Simulation-only force histories are summarized without reading unreported numerical values from curves; Fig. 8 is the more complete geometry sweep.
Irreplaceable uneven/curved geometry, delta-y sweep, contact-force evolution and force-space boundary; full axes, legends and caption visually verified.
Direct unit-test evidence for load-drag-retraction, progressive detachment, slip-distance saturation and six-surface ranking; full axes, legends, error bars and caption visually verified.
Demonstration photographs and labeled masses are preserved as lower-bound context; they do not supply force-displacement or repeatability data.

omitted_sections: Author contributions, funding and institutional statements

uncertain_items.detail: Fig. 2b labels R=20-40 um, while the body text states about 40-60 um. Neither range was selected as authoritative.

The paper calls the matrix 'diagonal symmetric' despite nonzero off-diagonal terms. The third-row translational coupling entries are printed with N/rad, which is not directly compatible with Mtheta per translational displacement without an unstated generalized-coordinate convention.
The figure shows +x left and +y up, while the example Fn arrow is downward. The paper does not fully state whether Fn is signed along +y or reported by normal-load magnitude.
The statement that average rough-peak spacing generally does not exceed 2 mm is not backed by surface metrology in this paper; 0-2 mm slip is a model constraint, not measured terrain statistics.
The figure contains error bars, but the paper does not state repeat count, error-bar definition or raw data. No exact values were digitized.
The surface ranking discussion refers to a previous probability model but does not formulate or validate that model in this paper; it is not treated as a source equation.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.12, Fig. 10 and supplementary-material notice
uncertain_items.detail: The PDF references supplementary videos S1-S3, but those files are not present in the workspace. Static photographs and stated masses were checked; dynamic behavior was not independently reviewed.

known_warnings: The PDF text layer contains duplicated peer-review/final-layout text on several pages; all retained equations, values, figures and captions were checked against rendered pages rather than trusted from extraction order.
The rigid-flexible model analyzes one symmetric prong and extrapolates to the eight-unit gripper; it does not solve nonuniform unit contact, internal preload or asymmetric surface load sharing.
Hinge friction is ignored and the spine-tip contact/slip boundary is prescribed; there is no local 3D normal, friction cone, asperity fracture or measured terrain model.
Eq. (2) must not be directly coded until generalized-coordinate units and force signs are reconciled.
The 5 mm towing distance is a paper-specific experimental recommendation without reported sample count or cross-surface universality.
Whole-gripper capacities (>60 N normal, >30 N tangential) are reported without repeatability statistics and must remain prototype lower-bound evidence.
Surface labels and qualitative morphology are not measurements of roughness, friction or material strength.
Poppler reported missing display fonts for Symbol and ArialUnicode, but all 14 rendered pages and the three direct PDF clips were visually legible.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows and image-link rows; within the allowed 3500-character extension because the paper contains independent single-flexure, ten-spine-unit and whole-gripper layers.
